name: Barranco del Cautivo, Oasis and Rambla de Tabernas – circular through the Desierto de Tabernas
name_original: Barranco del Cautivo, Oasis y Rambla de Tabernas – circular por el Desierto de Tabernas
region_intro: Jogging in Desierto De Tabernas offers a unique experience through Europe's only true desert landscape. The region is characterized by arid terrain, dry riverbeds (ramblas), and distinctive barrancos (ravines). Runners can expect routes that navigate through a rugged environment, occasionally encountering sparse vegetation and geological formations. The trails provide opportunities to explore the desert's unique ecosystem and its quiet, expansive vistas. Best jogging routes in Desierto De Tabernas The most popular jogging route is Barranco del Cautivo, Oasis and Rambla de Tabernas – circular through the Desierto de Tabernas, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores ravines, an oasis, and dry riverbeds within the desert. Another top favourite among local runners is Puntal loop from Tabernas, a difficult 17.5 miles (28.2 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain, offering extensive views across the desert landscape. Local runners also love the Running loop from Tabernas, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through the desert's varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging in Desierto De Tabernas is defined by arid landscapes, barrancos, and ramblas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging running routes. The routes in Desierto De Tabernas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 130 runners have used komoot to explore Desierto De Tabernas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: There are over 20 running routes available in Desierto De Tabernas, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore this unique desert landscape.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: Yes, Desierto De Tabernas offers some easier running options. While the terrain can be rugged, there is at least one designated easy route and several moderate trails that beginners might find manageable, especially if they are looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are the typical distances for running routes in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: Running routes in Desierto De Tabernas vary significantly in length. You can find shorter loops around 5 miles (8 km), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985139707/barranco-del-cautivo-oasis-and-rambla-de-tabernas-circular-through-the-desierto-de-tabernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo, Oasis and Rambla de Tabernas – circular through the Desierto de Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;, up to more challenging long-distance runs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal loop from Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 17.5 miles (28.2 km).

Q: Are there challenging running trails for experienced runners in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: Absolutely. Desierto De Tabernas is home to 11 difficult running routes, perfect for experienced runners seeking a challenge. These trails often feature significant elevation gain and rugged terrain, offering extensive views across the desert. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986487668/cordel-de-la-solana-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordel de la Solana Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: The scenery in Desierto De Tabernas is truly unique, characterized by arid terrain, dry riverbeds (ramblas), and distinctive barrancos (ravines). You'll encounter geological formations, sparse desert vegetation, and expansive, quiet vistas. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985139707/barranco-del-cautivo-oasis-and-rambla-de-tabernas-circular-through-the-desierto-de-tabernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo, Oasis and Rambla de Tabernas – circular through the Desierto de Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;, even lead to an oasis.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Desierto De Tabernas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985139707/barranco-del-cautivo-oasis-and-rambla-de-tabernas-circular-through-the-desierto-de-tabernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo, Oasis and Rambla de Tabernas – circular through the Desierto de Tabernas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Beyond the stunning desert landscapes, you can discover several interesting sights. Look out for natural formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo&lt;/a&gt; and various &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3564584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;desert canyon views&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes might even pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3394954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuerte El Cóndor Film Set Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, a nod to the region's cinematic history, or offer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Lawrence of Arabia Oasis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: The running trails in Desierto De Tabernas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Runners often praise the unique, expansive desert environment, the quiet solitude, and the variety of terrain, from dry riverbeds to dramatic barrancos. Many appreciate the opportunity to explore Europe's only true desert.

Q: Is Desierto De Tabernas suitable for family-friendly running or jogging?
A: While the desert environment can be challenging, some of the moderate routes, particularly those with shorter distances and less elevation gain, could be suitable for families with older children who are accustomed to running. Always check the route details for difficulty and length before heading out with family.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas like Desierto De Tabernas, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety in the rugged terrain. Always carry plenty of water for both yourself and your dog, especially in this arid environment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Desierto De Tabernas?
A: The best time for running in Desierto De Tabernas is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be extremely hot, making running challenging and potentially unsafe due to high temperatures and intense sun. Always check the weather forecast and plan your runs for early morning or late afternoon during warmer periods.

Q: What should I wear when running in Desierto De Tabernas, especially in summer?
A: In Desierto De Tabernas, especially during warmer months, it's essential to wear lightweight, breathable, and light-colored clothing to reflect the sun. A wide-brimmed hat, sunglasses, and sunscreen are crucial. Sturdy trail running shoes with good grip are recommended for the varied and sometimes loose terrain. Always carry ample water, regardless of the season.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the running routes?
A: The Desierto De Tabernas is a largely uninhabited natural area, so direct access to cafes or refreshment points along the trails is limited. It's best to carry all necessary water and snacks with you. The nearest amenities, including cafes and shops, can be found in the nearby town of Tabernas, which serves as a common starting point for many routes.
tips: Very good to drive sand gravel path

The "Barranco del Cautivo" is located in the Tabernas desert and departs from the parking lot of "Mini-Hollywood". This gritty area of gritty rock, as well as the western-style film villages surrounding it, has been the scene of some blockbuster hits such as Once upon a time in the West, Zorro, The Good, the bad and the ugly and Asterix & Obelix.
